title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 1 (Fragment 3)
text:
“I don’t think the Casa Petrocchi can be anything like this inside.”

Before Tonino could agree, they were swooped on lovingly by Aunt Maria, who was fatter than Aunt Gina, but not as fat as Aunt Anna. “Where have you been, my loves? I’ve been ready for your lessons for half an hour or more!”

Everyone in the Casa Montana worked very hard. Paolo and Tonino were already being taught the first rules for making spells. When Aunt Maria was busy, they were taught by their father, Antonio. Antonio was the eldest son of Old Niccolo, and would be head of the Casa Montana when Old Niccolo died. Paolo thought this weighed on his father. Antonio was a thin, worried person who laughed less often than the other Montanas. He was different. One of the differences was that, instead of letting Old Niccolo carefully choose a wife for him from a spell-house in Italy, Antonio had gone on a visit to England and come back married to Elizabeth. Elizabeth taught the boys music.

“If I’d been teaching that Angelica Petrocchi,” she was fond of saying, “she’d never have turned anything green.”

Old Niccolo said Elizabeth was the best musician in Caprona. And that, Lucia told the boys, was why Antonio got away with marrying her. But Rosa told them to take no notice. Rosa was proud of being half English.

Paolo and Tonino were probably prouder to be Montanas. It was a grand thing to know you were born into a family that was known world-wide as the greatest spell-house in Europe—if you did not count the Petrocchis. There were times when Paolo could hardly wait to grow up and be like his cousin, dashing Rinaldo. Everything came easily to Rinaldo. Girls fell in love with him, spells dripped from his pen. He had composed seven new charms before he left school. And these days, as Old Niccolo said, making a new spell was not easy. There were so many already. Paolo admired Rinaldo desperately. He told Tonino that Rinaldo was a true Montana.

Tonino agreed, because he was more than a year younger than Paolo and valued Paolo’s opinions, but it always seemed to him that it was Paolo who was the true Montana. Paolo was as quick as Rinaldo. He could learn without trying spells which took Tonino days to acquire. Tonino was slow. He could only remember things if he went over them again and again. It seemed to him that Paolo had been born with an instinct for magic which he just did not have himself.

Tonino was sometimes quite depressed about his slowness. Nobody else minded in the least. All his sisters, even the studious Corinna, spent hours helping him. Elizabeth assured him he never sang out of tune. His father scolded him for working too hard, and Paolo assured him that he would be streets ahead of the other children when he went to school. Paolo had just started school. He was as quick at ordinary lessons as he was at spells.

But when Tonino started school, he was just as slow there as he was at home. School bewildered him. He did not understand what the teachers wanted him to do. By the first Saturday he was so miserable that he had to slip away from the Casa and wander around Caprona in tears. He was missing for hours.

vocabulary.definition: To learn or develop a skill, habit, or quality.
vocabulary.example: Spells which took Tonino days to acquire.

vocabulary.definition: To tell someone confidently that something is true or will happen.
vocabulary.example: Paolo assured him he would be streets ahead at school.

vocabulary.definition: Confused and puzzled completely.
vocabulary.example: School bewildered him.

vocabulary.definition: With great attention to detail or safety.
vocabulary.example: Old Niccolo carefully chose a wife for him.

vocabulary.definition: Magical spells or objects believed to have power.
vocabulary.example: Spells dripped from his pen, and he composed new charms.

vocabulary.definition: To pick out or select from a number of alternatives.
vocabulary.example: Old Niccolo chose a wife for Antonio.

vocabulary.definition: To create or write (a piece of music or literary work).
vocabulary.example: He had composed seven new charms before he left school.

vocabulary.definition: Attractive, energetic, and confident in appearance or behaviour.
vocabulary.example: His cousin, dashing Rinaldo.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows great urgency or need.
vocabulary.example: Paolo admired Rinaldo desperately.

vocabulary.definition: Having a liking or affection for someone or something.
vocabulary.example: She was fond of saying...

vocabulary.definition: Impressive in size, appearance, or style; important or noble.
vocabulary.example: It was a grand thing to know you belonged to that family.

vocabulary.definition: The person in charge of an organization or group.
vocabulary.example: Antonio would be head of the Casa Montana.

vocabulary.definition: An innate, typically fixed pattern of behaviour in animals in response to certain stimuli.
vocabulary.example: Paolo had been born with an instinct for magic.

vocabulary.definition: Very unhappy or uncomfortable.
vocabulary.example: He was so miserable that he wandered around in tears.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling deep pleasure or satisfaction as a result of achievements or qualities.
vocabulary.example: Rosa was proud of being half English.

vocabulary.definition: Feeling pleased and satisfied about something you or someone close to you has done.
vocabulary.example: Paolo and Tonino were probably prouder to be Montanas.

vocabulary.definition: To reprimand or speak angrily to someone for their mistakes.
vocabulary.example: His father scolded him for working too hard.

vocabulary.definition: A house or institution where magical spells are taught or created.
vocabulary.example: Known worldwide as the greatest spell-house in Europe.

vocabulary.definition: Spending a lot of time studying or reading.
vocabulary.example: Even studious Corinna spent hours helping him.

vocabulary.definition: To move quickly downwards or suddenly approach, often to grab or surround someone.
vocabulary.example: They were swooped on lovingly by Aunt Maria.

vocabulary.definition: To have instructed or educated someone.
vocabulary.example: Paolo and Tonino were taught the first rules for making spells.

vocabulary.definition: To walk slowly around or to various places without a specific purpose or direction.
vocabulary.example: He had to slip away from the Casa and wander around Caprona.

vocabulary.definition: Feelings of anxiety or concern.
vocabulary.example: Antonio was a thin, worried person.
